Hey everyone, this is Russell Brunson. Welcome back to the 10X Secrets Masterclass. Now today, I'm excited for this lesson because a lot of you guys I know who have been learning about this stuff and you're still kind of figuring out like how is this gonna actually work for my business? So maybe thinking like Russell, I'm not ever gonna go on a seminar stage. Maybe you don't have a desire to stand in front of 100 or a thousand or 10,000 people or maybe just your business, that's not really an opportunity, which I understand that there's a lot of businesses where there's not big events happening all the time. And so for you guys who that's not like, you're like, how is this gonna work for me? I wanna tell you the path that I went down so you can see how this is gonna work for you guys online as well. In fact, when I started my speaking career, this is man, probably 13, 14 years ago now. My twins, I have twin boys who were just born, they were super young. And I remember I was traveling every weekend going out, you know, flying out, doing the stage, saying make money, flying back home. And it was kind of like going out and killing something for your family and bringing it back, right? And it was making really good money doing it. But honestly, it got lonely. I was staying in hotel rooms by myself. I was going and traveling places. I didn't really know people. It wasn't super enjoyable. I enjoyed standing on stage and speaking and selling them. The rest of the time was just loneliness, if I'm completely honest. I remember about two years into it, I was actually at the Boise Airport. It was like 11.39, it was about to fly out. And I was the only person in the airport. I'm sitting there waiting for the plane. I remember just feeling like, why am I doing this? My wife's home with kids. Like, I could be sitting at home with them, instead I'm here in this airport alone. And I was like, there's gotta be a better way. And I remember texting my wife and I was like, I'm gonna quit speaking. And she texts back, she's like, you can't. Like, that's how we make our money. I'm like, I know, but it's just not worth it anymore. And so just like that, I went and flew out. The last thing I spoke and the whole flight there and whole flight home, I'm like, how do I replicate this online, because I'm gonna try to figure out how to do it from the comfort of my own home. So I can be with my wife and be with my kids and go to bed at night in my own bed. And that's kind of a scary thing when you're like, this is my income. And for me, this is before webinars had come out. It was back when people were doing tele-seminars. Everything happened on the phones. And I remember I started trying to do these tele-seminars so I would promote it and get a bunch of people to register and I'd get on the phone. And it was similar, I would give my stage presentation on phone, but it didn't quite work the same. There's little intricacies and tweaks and changes I had to make. And same thing happened a few years later when webinars started coming. I remember, go to webinar came out, we started testing that. And I started getting better and better of that. Since then till now, I've literally done hundreds and hundreds and hundreds of webinars online. And I still, I like webinars more than state selling because you have the ability to sit and come from your home, instead of getting 100 people in the number 500 people in the room, you can get a thousand or 5,000 or more. In fact, we've had webinars that have had tens of thousands of people on it. And it's nice because you can close how much it sells then you go back home to bed at night. And it's really, really cool. And so I wanna kinda walk you through that process and how it works. Now, webinars, they're nice because they're very, very similar to a stage presentation. You know, tell seminars a little bit different. You have to tweak things because people aren't visually watching things. But for webinars, it's nice because you have your slides, you can put your camera on, people are able to interact with you, which is really good. The hardest part about it though is like when you're in a room and you're on stage, you see 9,000 people. There's a certain like energy level you get from the audience that's not there when you're speaking in front of a webinar screen. And so for some of you guys, you're gonna experience that where you stand up there and you're like, I'm talking to myself. And it's really awkward and weird. And so when the biggest thing you have to learn is like, how do I pretend like I'm standing in front of an audience with a thousand people? And you have to kind of trick your mind almost to like put yourself in that state of like, okay, I'm in a bunch of people, I'm talking. You know, for me, I have a standing desk, so I raise my desk, so I'm actually standing instead of sitting. Cause there's a different energy level of like, hey, this is Russell, welcome to the webinar. Versus me standing, like, hey, this is Russell. And so the more you can kind of mimic if you being on stage, the better your energy level is gonna come off. Cause if you come off talking normal like this on your PowerPoint slides and your presentation, you're gonna sound like you're sound asleep, right? You gotta be able to level 10 all the time and it comes off normal when you're actually speaking. Like if you see me doing a live webinar, I'm yelling, I'm screaming, I'm moving my hands. Like I'm talking to the audience, like I'm pretending there's people there. Like I'm asking, I'm doing my same trial closes. Like all this stuff we've talked about in the training, I'm doing the same things there and I'm getting the people to participate at home. And so we have to understand like, those things are very, very similar. Even though they seem different, like they're actually very similar. You gotta keep doing them. I keep asking people trial closes. I keep asking people, yes, I try to get people to raise their hand. Like I'm doing all the same things cause I'm envisioning them doing them from home. And so that's kind of the first thing to understand. Now on a walkie-talkie, I just do what a basic webinar funnel looks like. And there's kind of two different ways to do webinar funnels. The first way is to do a live webinar. And for everybody, especially if you listen to the interview with Kailin, you know, I recommend doing a live webinar at least once a week until you've made about a million dollars in that funnel. And then at that point you can automate, you can shift things up and do an automated webinar. I'm gonna go over kind of both funnel types to understand them. But the biggest key is like doing it live over and over and over and over again. And so that's the key. So I'm gonna talk about the live webinar funnel first and then we'll show you kind of the tweaks you make if and when you decide to go and automate it. Okay, all right, so the first thing is if you look at an actual webinar, the first key is the registration page, right? Now if you're in a live event, there's no registration page. The promoters already got them in the room, they're sitting there and you've got their attention, which is the nicest thing about a live event. Online you don't have their attention, right? You're buying ads on Facebook, they're scrolling through, they click on an ad and they come over to your funnel and they have a registration page. Now you've got this opportunity to try to capture them and get their attention. Now the keys that I found on a webinar registration page, the number one most important thing is curiosity. Curiosity will first off get people to register and second off it'll get people to actually show up. Okay, if you don't have a lot of curiosity, what'll happen is some people register but they don't show up because they're like, oh, I think I know what's happening. In fact, Mike and AJ who are in my inner circle, this webinar campaign they built out and they came to my inner circle, meaning they're like, hey, we have this campaign and it's not doing very well, it's costing, I think it was costing like $20 per lead to get someone to register for the webinar and the show up rate was like 15%. And I looked at the page and the landing page was the problem. I was like, I know exactly what your problem is, like what's the problem? And you look at the landing page at the time, it was very, you could read the headline and you kind of knew what it was about. It was like, how do you use reputation to land clients into whatever in your local area, right? So I see that and I'm like, oh, it's about reputation management. And the problem is like, if I think I can answer the question by looking at the headline, then I'm not likely to actually register for the class because I think I already know what it is. Or maybe I'll register, but I'm not likely to show up and I'm like, oh, I probably know, like I think I know what it is, it's about reputation, right? So all the people you're getting are people who don't know what that thing is. And so what I told them, I said, you just need to tweak it and cause more curiosity. Don't tell them what it is, tell them what it's not. So they changed their headline and it was like the number one way to get local clients in your area. By the way, it's not PPC, it's not Facebook ads, it's not SEO, it's not, da, da, da, da, da, right? And there's all these things that was not. So they didn't tell them what it was, they told them what it was not. And their registration, they're costing them from like $20 per register down to like $5, which is crazy. So they cut their costs in by three times. And then their show up rate went from like a 15% to like a 25% because more people were curious. So the cost went down, show up rate went up. So whenever people are like, oh, my webinar's not working, it's costing too much of a leader, nobody's showing up. It's usually a curiosity thing, okay? So the headline of your landing page should be super curiosity based. Now usually I just have a headline and a registration box. I don't have a video, I don't have anything because all those things tell too much and it gets people thinking they know the answer and they don't show up. So for me, like my registration page are just the headline, the title of my presentation, which is much raw curiosity as possible, right? They opt in there, now they've registered for the webinar, okay? Now, typically what's gonna happen is all these pages you're gonna build out inside of ClickFunnels. In fact, in the members area down below, I'm gonna give you guys a live webinar funnel and also an automated webinar funnel that's already prebuilt. You can just click on a button, it'll pull into your account and you can go in there and just change out the headlines, change out the images and make them your own. But with the live webinar, the one thing that's different is that you're using a third party service to actually host the webinar. The two that I recommend, one is called Go To Webinar and that's one I use for the first 10 years of my career and the second one is called Zoom and Zoom's the one I use right now. They're both amazing, they both work great, but basically you're gonna run the actual webinar in these classrooms that are run by the Go To Webinar Zoom and basically you just integrate it with ClickFunnels. So when someone registers for your web class right here, it'll then push them into your Zoom room and now you've got them here so you can follow up them into your funnel but then the class actually happens through that third party server, does that make sense? Okay, so they come in here, they come into your funnel, they give you the email address and then boom, they're added to either Zoom or Go To Webinar, which everyone makes sense for you. Okay, so they're added to that account and then over here what we do is we start this process. Okay, and this is called the follow-up funnel and I'll talk about this here in a minute, but they register, they're added to Go To Webinar Zoom then put into a follow-up funnel and from here they're taken to a thank you page. Thank you. Okay, so thank you page, the goal of the thank you page is a little bit different. The goal of the registration page is curiosity and then the goal of the thank you page is to get them so excited about what it is you're doing and why they should be there that they actually show up. So typically on the thank you page, I do do a video and on this video I'm just coming out with as much energy and excitement to get them pumped about things. A lot of times I'll show my slides in the back, like I'm working on the presentation, this is what it looks like and I'll show them some of the slides, trying to build curiosity, excitement, passion, I'll make sure case studies, testimonials, but I'm just trying to get them as pumped and as excited as possible because I want them to feel like what it's gonna be like to be on the webinar and I want them to crave and desire that feeling, okay? So that's what the thank you page is all about. I'm also gonna put on the thank you page, like the dates, the times, all the information so they know and they can put on their calendar, much information is to make sure they show up as possible, okay? So that's the key there. And that's the thank you page. Now somewhere after the thank you page, somewhere over here is going to be the actual webinar that you are giving, okay? So again, this is gonna be an either zoom or go to webinar. So typically when I happen, let's say this live event is happening on a Thursday, right? So I might spend Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and half a day Thursday driving ads into this thing. They come, they register, they have the thank you page and this is getting excited for the webinar that's gonna be happening the next day or two days from now or three days from now depending on when they came into the funnel and it's all about getting them and pumped and excited for that, right? Okay, so that's basically what the funnel looks like so far, okay, it's very simple. Now this right here is called a follow-up funnel. So follow-up funnel is like all the things I'm gonna do to try to indoctrinate them and persuade them and get them excited about showing up on the actual webinar, okay? So what we typically do is depending here, if you get just their email address, you can email them. If you add a Facebook Messenger opt-in, then you can get their email and their Facebook Messenger so you can actually be sending a message from Facebook Messenger reminding you about the webinar. You can also add text message. There's other things you can add here as well to gather information so you can then start pushing things to people. And a lot of people do different things. You can get as simple or as complex as you want. For the intents of this training, I'm just gonna show you what to do with email but it's very powerful and very smart to weave in like a Facebook Messenger bot, right? For example, you could have somebody register and send some emails out and then like 10 minutes before the webinar starts, send them a Facebook Messenger and be like, here's the link, go log in right now, get started, which is super powerful way to increase your show up rates, right? We're sending them a text message 30 minutes before it starts. Hey, in 30 minutes of starting, be sure to log in. Okay, so these are some of the ways you can be thinking about how to get people to actually show up, okay? All right, so right here is after they've registered, I create three, I usually call them indoctrination pages. Garrett White calls them preseduction pages, right? It's the same thing, indoctrinating, seducing them, getting them excited about this thing that you're doing, right? And so what these pages are typically is me on video telling stories about why they should be on the webinar, what's gonna be happening, what they're gonna learn, what they're gonna discover, case studies about someone else who went through this web class, what they learned, right? All the things I'm doing to pride a presweet them to get them excited about what's gonna be happening, right? Like a Grant Cardone's event, if you noticed before, I spoke earlier in the event, he got up on stage, he was like, hey guys, later today there's this guy speaking, he's Russell Brunson, he's got a $100 million company, he's indoctrinating, he's presuading, and getting excited to actually show up to my session, right? Because some people, they don't show up to the session, like, oh, I don't know who this kid is, I'm not gonna come. So anything you can do to get the people excited, the more, the better, right? So for me, this one comes in here and there's an email, it says, hey, go check out this page to learn the number one secret about blah. Or check out this page, I got a free case study about blah. And all these things are just presuasion pages, and all they do is they're getting people more excited about this webinar, okay? Just like Thank You page is getting them excited about the webinar, these are just getting people excited about the webinar. And one of the big concerns people always tell me is like, Russ, what happens if, you know, Monday they come in and they get this one on, you know, Monday, this one Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday, watch the webinar, it works perfectly, but what happens if they register on Wednesday? And Wednesday they get it, and then Wednesday they get this, and then Thursday it's the webinar, and then Friday and Saturday they get these, and they start freaking out, and they're all concerned, and people seem to stress so much about this. Now, we have to understand about the presuasion, or the preseduction, or the indoctrination page, whatever you want to call it, it doesn't matter where they watch these, right? These are amplifiers, these are to amplify them, either get on the webinar to buy your offer, whatever it is, right? So don't stress if it's like, what if they, they're not gonna get, they're only gonna get two of these before the webinar starts, or only one before the webinar starts, it doesn't matter. These are all about just like increasing the temperature, getting more excited, getting them more present, okay? I don't care if they watch the webinar, and number two comes out, that may get them to be more likely to buy, or maybe the thing that pushes them over the edge to watch the replay, whatever that might happen, okay? So that's how this is typically working. This is the base part of the webinar, okay? So then, some registers, they get these indoct, they hit the thank you page, they start getting indoctrination emails, indoctrinating, pre-suading about what's happening on the webinar, and then this webinar happens, right? So the goal is to do the webinar, give as many people on as possible, then the webinar happens, and you do your whole presentation, everything we talked about in the Tennis Seekers Masterclass, it's usually, typically, if you watch how your presentation is gonna be, it's gonna be like a 90 minute presentation, so if you look at it, the first 10, 15 minutes is you introducing yourself, right? Then you got secret number one, right here, secret number two, secret number three, that takes up the first hour, and about the hours we're transitioning now to the close, the stack and the close, right? So when I'm looking at my clock, I know about the top of the hours, like cool, this is normally like, hey, let me ask you guys a question real quick, right? And that's where I transition to the close, and then I spend the next 30 minutes going through the stack and the close, okay? So the next 30 minutes is the stack and the close. Now this is something that I do different on webinars, I don't do it live event. In webinars, people are captivated, and during the stack and the close, you got 30 minutes where you're closing, and you're doing all the stuff, you're driving into the URL, if you remember in the Tennis Seekers, I talked about telling people, like stand up and go to the back, like stand up, you have my permission, go to the back, like run the table. I'm doing the same thing on the webinar, so if I'm saying, okay, right now, open a new browser window, go to www.clickfunnels.com. And I'm telling them exactly what to do. So instead of having to stand up and go to the back of the room, I'm actually telling them, and I tell them, I'm specifically saying, open up another browser, sometimes they may be watching, sometimes people are clicking the link on the screen, it doesn't work, you know, because they're on a webinar or whatever, but I tell them, open up another browser, open Chrome, Firefox, whatever you use, open that up, and then go to the top of the bar, go to www.clickfunnels.com. Go right now to get the special offer, right? So I'm telling them what to do all throughout this thing, and then at the end of the 90 minutes, this is typically where your stage presentation ends, right? People run, music comes up, and you're done. But on a webinar, people are still there, and a lot of people have bought, but what I found is that, for the, you know, people will stay on for as long as you're there. In fact, I've done webinars up to four hours before in the past, and people will stay for the entire four hours, not everybody, you'll see people dropping throughout the whole thing, but people will stay. So for me, I'm like, they're captivated, I've done all my closing, what I usually do at the end of 30 minutes, I say, okay, I'm gonna get a drink real quick, I can drink water, whoo, I'm usually your beat up by that time, because during the stack of my clothes, you're going really fast, and I say, okay, I'm gonna answer Q and A's right now for the next 30 minutes or so. And I like open it, I have a huge countdown clock on my slides, it's a 30 minute countdown clock, and for 30 minutes, I just do Q and A's. Now I don't let people ask the Q and A's live, because if I do that sometimes it throws me off, sometimes you'll get a heckler and like messes up your brain. So what I do instead is I have pre-written Q and A's that are sitting there, and the Q and A's are there in this little, I actually pre-write them out on my slides, and I say, okay, first question I have is this, and I open it and this question pops up, and then I use it kind of like, if I was on Home Shopping Network, or you know Home Shopping Network, where they've got the 1-800 number on the bottom, and they're like, they talk about something, and then they call the action, they talk about something, call the action, talk about something, call the action for the entire show. So the same thing here is like, I answer a question, so somebody asks a question, I answer it, and usually it's a question that I know is gonna help push over another segment of the market. Some people aren't buying because of this question, or this question, and so I think I have, I think I have probably 30 or so questions that I've pre-loaded in my slides, and so I answer the question, and then I come back and do the call the action, okay? So there's the answer to the question, and you guys who are on the fence, you're wondering if you get started, now's the time to get started, go to www.clickphones.com.com. Slash go to get started now. Okay, question number two, and I do question number two, and I come back, okay, and I go, I keep doing that for 30, 34 minutes, okay? And what happens for me, a lot of times that 30 minutes, I'll close another 25 to 50% of the sales I did during the stack and the close. So it's worth staying on. So my webinar's typically now about two hours long where I'm doing this, and this is happening, this is Q&A, and the other thing, if you watch the Katelyn's webinar, she talked a little bit about this. In fact, I will include in that section of her, of the training I'll put in there, the podcast episode I did about this, but it's the thing we learned from Jason Flavoli when he says, you're probably thinking this, right? And we start asking questions like that, like, hey, if a lot of you guys who are sitting on the fence, you're probably thinking this, right? And then the answer to resolve the concern. You're probably thinking this, right? And then resolve the concern. You're probably thinking this, right? Resolve the concern. So it's Q&A's, it's your, you're probably thinking this, right? Questions, and you keep doing those all through 30 minutes. You're just closing, closing, closing, closing, closing, closing, closing, closing, closing, closing, closing. And by the end of the two hours, you have closed insane amounts of people. I wish that I could do that at live events. I wish the most promoters would give me two hours to keep doing those, because I could continue to close like crazy. But typically most promoters only give you about 90 minutes on stage, just kind of the max. So, but on a webinar, it's your own platform, you can go as long as you want. So keep doing Q&A's. I used to do them for 10 minutes, I had a 10 minute countdown clock. What I saw is that sales kept coming in till the clock hits zero and the sales stopped. And then I was like, crap. So then I went to a 15 minute countdown clock and sales kept going for all 15 minutes and the clock hit zero, they stopped. So I went to a 30 minute, about 30 minutes was my sweet spot where I kind of, I kind of found my sweet spot for that. And so I do 30 minutes and that clock keeps running, writing all the way down to zero. And people always ask me like, what happens to the clock at zero? And the answer for me is nothing. Like it's just, it's, I never tell them when the clock at zero, they offer is gone. I just have this clock here which causes this like stress and turmoil of people like, I gotta buy because like, the clock's almost zero. And so I just let it keep running until it's zero. And then I usually wrap the webinar up at that point. Okay. And so that's kind of how I structure. So those are some of the tweaks and the changes that happen between a webinar versus live presenting live. But other than that, it's almost the same. Okay. All right. So that's how we structure the webinar. Now when the webinar is done, this is where this fall up sequence comes back, right? So now the webinar is over. And what's gonna typically happen when you do this, you might get anywhere from 20 to 30% who are gonna show up live. Okay. So that means 70, 80% of people who registered here who wanted it didn't show up for whatever reason. Okay. So now it's like, now I have this time where I gotta try to close all the people who didn't sign up or people who may not have bought live on the webinar, right? And so for me, typically it's like Monday, like I'm promoting this if we look at like dates, right? I'll put this in red. So this is like Monday through Thursday. Over here, and typically I do my live webinars on Thursday. I just found for me that's usually the best day. And what's happening next is like Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. And typically what happens to me is I close this off for Sunday at midnight. Okay. All right. So then what's gonna happen is, and if you look at, I talked about some of the other presentations, but for me, I'm moving people. I'm trying to, initially I try to close people emotionally and then logically and then urgency and scarcity. So if I take this right here, I lay it upon it. This is like, this is my emotional close, right? So I'll put a person here, I'm gonna draw little tears because this is like the emotional close, right? Of the webinars, trying to get them to buy here. So this is emotion. D-I-O-N. As I move through this sequence, I start transitioning more to logic. Okay, so for logic, I'm gonna put on like a college graduation caps. This makes me feel like I'm, there you go. There's my doodle. There's a graduation clap, right? So here I'm moving towards logic and then here towards the end, I start moving towards, here's the worried guy and this is urgency and scarcity. Okay. All right, so obviously, emotionally, maybe I shouldn't make him smile, maybe I'm kind of like in the middle here too, like, oh, I'm emotionally engaged. Okay, so if you do the presentation the way I taught you guys in the Ten X Secrets Masterclass, it's been this emotional experience, right? They're like, their beliefs have been broken, all these false belief patterns, also they're looking at the world through this new lens and it's like, oh my gosh, if I really want the result I'm looking for, like I need to shift vehicles from whatever this is to this new vehicle and like, it's in a very emotional cell, right? And so the majority of your cells come during this emotional cell, right? And this is gonna be your highest percentage. For me, I usually look at how many people are still on at the hour mark when I transition to my stack and my close, okay? So at the top of the hour, I was looking at the thing and I said, okay, there's 300 people. And my goal is from how many people are on during the stack and the close, I wanna close. I always tell people this is the numbers, like 5% you've got a six figure year webinar 10%, you're a multi, you're a seven figure year webinar. If you get 15%, like this is like eight plus figure, like this is like click phones is 15%, okay? So that's kind of the ratio. Most people, if you get to about 10%, that's a million dollar year webinar at least, okay? And so that's kind of the metrics I'm looking for. So I look at how many people are on at the top of the hour for I transition to stack and the close and then I always write that number down. And for me, I'm always within 15% of that number is like where I always hit. So I know, so at the top of the hour, like the number, boom, there's 500 people, there's 1,000 people. And exactly how much money I'm gonna make almost to the dollar. Like it's really strange how close it is. It doesn't typically deviate. It does sometimes based on some things with most parts pretty close, right? So that's kind of the thing. So the highest percentage, like 10, 15% is like, that's of the people who are focusing, like that's the emotional buyers. Like that's gonna be the biggest percentage right now. I'm taking the other 70, 80% and put them through this rest of the process. And I'm trying to now logically close them, urgency and scarcity and things like that, okay? So for me, now what I do now is then I come over here and this is now the replay page. And so this page is gonna show a replay of what happened on the live webinar, right? So typically when you're doing it through Zoom or go to webinar, you can record it. When it's done, take the recording, upload it to YouTube or WISD or whatever and I embed that video here on a ClickFunnels page. All right, and there's a button here to buy the thing. And so the next set of emails will come back here. So here's my first one, I'm still working emotionally. So it's like, hey, you may miss the webinar because maybe you had a date night or you couldn't get out or whatever. Now's the time to go and actually go and watch the replay, right? So I let them go watch the replay. So that's kind of my first email coming out. Then I start transitioning now more towards logic. So here a lot of times what'll happen is maybe I'll have a page and if you watched my interview with Myron and the closing seekers if you guys bought the upsell, we talked about the repitch, right? So the repitch is all about the logical closing. How do I logically close these people, right? So I'll do a lot of times, I'll have a video here. And what works really good for logical ones is a Q and A session, right? So the second email here will be like, hey, I had a lot of people who attended the webinar and had tons of really good questions that I thought, you know, if one person's asking is probably 10 or more have it, I'm gonna go through these questions, right? And so I find all the logical arguments people are asking and I do a live video here or a video of me answering questions. So I might just have a paste paper like, hey, question number one, people are asking is, is why should I be doing this right now? So let me explain logically why this makes sense for you to do right now and then I'll answer, right? And question number two is this, let me explain logically why you should be doing this. And I just logically explain all the different concerns why people may not have said yes so far. And what I'll do from there is from this video, then I do the Q and A, Q and A, and then from here, I push people back to the replay page. Okay, so you hear all the, here's all logical things now. If you're ready to get started, go back, watch the replay if you haven't yet, or you can go get started right now and I push them back to go and buy. Okay, so this replay page happens in ClickFunnels, the logic close Q and A page happens in ClickFunnels, both are pushing to this, okay? So I'm shifting from emotion to logic. And then typically what happens the last day in my sequence, so this is for me, it's usually Sunday. Sunday at midnight usually what I do is I pull the offer down, so I pull this funnel down and I'll create a new one for next week. But I pull this funnel down so the offer disappears. So this is like the last day. So for Sunday, what I'll typically do is I'll usually send out multiple messages, at least two and sometimes three, right? So what might happen is Sunday morning, I might send out the first message, because message number one might be like, hey, today is it? Or just gonna scare you, it's disappearing tonight at midnight, just so you guys know. If you have quite, like I wanna go, I wanna give you all the links right now if you have some one link, go watch the replay right here, number two, if you're ready to buy, here's the link to go buy. And number three, if you're still on the fence, go watch my Q and A's, because this will answer your question, right? So I give them the call to actions, all three of the things that I've already, that I've already kind of given them here in the sequence, right? Then number two, I'm coming back and I'm really driving the urgency and scarcity now, right? So I'm saying, okay, this is the deal you guys, sorry, urgency and scarcity. This is going away really, really quick. I wanna make sure you have a chance to go and watch this and be part of it. I'll probably give the same links again, but I'm like, you got 12 hours left and 12 hours disappears. I don't want you yelling at me tomorrow when it's gone, so just go and do it really, really quick. And then 30 email is like two sentences. It's like, or just in scarcity again. But it's like, all right guys, three hours and cart closes, this is your last warning. Here's the links, thanks so much. Hope you enjoy your life, right? I remember it is, but it's really quick, really fast. Just cart closes, it's almost done, boom, that one goes out. And what's crazy is like that last email will get like 25% of sales come from the last three hours where people are like, ah, they freak out and they have to buy. And so that is kind of, that is the process, you guys. It's not super complicated, but when you understand the strategy, you see how it makes sense, like there's a registration page, there's three indoctrination pages, this is the thank you page. Then the webinar happens and go to webinar or Zoom wherever you do it. Then there's a replay page, and then there's a Q and A page where you're logically trying to close people. And that's kind of it. There's a couple emails going out here. You can do Facebook, or you can do Facebook Messenger, you can do text messages again. You can get as complex as you want, but for the most part, that's the framework of how we run our webinars, okay? And this is how, in case you're wondering, how we build ClickFunnels to a $100 million a year company was I was running these webinars every single week. Okay, I told you guys, if you listened to Katelyn's interview, I talked about doing a webinar live every single week. People always ask me, well, Russell, my audience is going to, like, I can't do a live every week because my audience will hear it every week and it's not gonna make any sense. And I said, no, you understand, this is not about doing this to your audience. This is about doing it to get a new audience, to build your following, to build your list, to build your, the people who are listening, right? So I'd build this funnel out and I'd start buying ads, right? I'd start getting new blood into my business and I'm buying ads from Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, I go through this process, boom, Thursday, the webinar, we make a bunch of sales, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, close it down, boom, that takes the webinar, this whole funnel, I close it down. What all we need to be doing is we'll clone it really quickly, close it down so it shuts down because I want real urgency and scarcity. This offer disappears for everyone in the funnel and I open the funnel again, new, a brand new one and then Monday I start driving new ads and new people into it, right? And I do that over and over, week in and week out, week in and week out, boom, keep doing it, keep doing it and keep doing it and this presentation will get better and better. You'll start at 5%, right? You export all the Q and A's, like hey, why are people getting stuck? You know, tweak the presentation, tweak it, tweak it and then next week you get it to 6%, right? And then I'm getting better, you export the Q and A's, what are the questions people are asking about the webinar? How do I tweak the presentation to resolve these concerns earlier? How do I put in a children's chunk here? How do I do this? How do I put a story and how do I do all these things? Let me add more trial closes then, right? You get next week, boom, you're at 7%, you do it again, boom, I'm at 8%, boom, I'm at 9%, boom, I'm at 10%, boom, I'm at 11, I'm at 12, you keep doing it and get better and better and better to the point where you can't increase it anymore, maybe you're stuck at 12%, you're stuck at 9%, whatever that is for you and you're like, I've done it 10 times in a row, I'm stuck at that spot, now it's like, okay, now that I've got it down, now I'm gonna make an automated version of this, right? Now you can take the presentation and take your best one, take that video, that recording of it, it's in the replay page and now you can make what we call an automated webinar funnel, okay? But the process is there, okay? The automated webinar funnel is almost the same, the only big difference between an automated webinar funnel and like an automated and a live webinar funnel is we're not using go to webinar or Zoom, okay? So what happens is I pull this piece out, right? And what happens is someone registers the webinar and then the thank you page, the webinar starts playing. Now there's an argument, and I've tested tons of stuff in the past, I've done it in the past where we try to mimic a live webinar, they register, thank you, do you wanna come to the Tuesday webinar or the Wednesday or the Thursday webinar, right? And that did good and then I remember one time we tested like watch yesterday's replay now, that was the option on the registration page, we found as a 50% of ourselves came people who watched yesterday's replay now, and I was like, oh, that's fascinating. So we tested like, okay, what if we did it where the webinar always starts in like three hours from now? And so we did that and that worked even better. And like what happens if the webinar starts in an hour from now, so we did, okay, and then the registered the live webinar starts in an hour and that did even better. And we showed it like, what if it did it every 15 minutes, every 10 minutes? And a sweet spot for us we have is like, when someone registers, it's basically the webinar starting in 10 minutes, ready? Go and they register and the next page it hits and then the webinar just starts. And for us right now that's what's been the best for us, okay? And so it's very simple, you're just taking out this one, it's almost like the replay page. You're making the replay page, the thank you page, the register, boom, his page, webinar starts. You still have the same indoctrination series goes out. You still have the emails that go out for people who missed it, you push them back to the replay page, you push them to a logical close page, urgency and scare, everything else happens the same. It's just taking the live element out of it, okay? But again, I highly recommend don't do that until the webinar is perfected. The problem if you don't, is you're gonna do the webinar the very first time, you're gonna have a 5% close rate. If next year, two years, five years, 10 years of your business, you're gonna be stuck in that thing. Whereas if you would have spent the time, put the effort in for a couple of weeks, or a couple of months up front, and you got to 10%, like you've now doubled everything that's coming through your business, right? You went from, you know, say, you have a business made two or $3 million a year, if you would put that time in, that same business would be doing five or $6 million a year, just by putting in the effort up front to increase the conversions. The conversions like the little hinges that swing these huge gates, and most people aren't willing to do the effort to do that, but when you do do that, it's life changing, right? ClickFunnels could have been a $20 million a year company, but it became $100 million because I got a point where it could have been 15% before I ever started automating it, does that make sense? And so that's kind of the process. Now, a couple other things. The 15%, this is what happens in a webinar. When you're on a live stage, that number is usually like double, right? Usually I'll get 20, 25, 30, I've had times when 50% or more in a room, it's easier in a room, because everyone's seeing that it had to listen to you. On a webinar, there's people like, they're catwalked in, their wife called them, their kids are, it's time for dinner, like there's so many distractions that can happen on a webinar, you don't have it in a room, so your clothes right in a room should always be higher, but on a webinar, 10% is like the sweet spot. If you can get to that point, you are gonna kill it. Okay, so one more thing I wanna add, and I think we'll be done with this session. The last thing I wanna add is, is people always ask, like when they start teaching this, and the expert secrets books the first time I kind of taught this process and said, this is how you should be doing it. And so people got really good, okay, I'm gonna do a webinar live once a week and start doing it. And so they go through this process, they're building lists, it's getting bigger, every single time it's happening, and they only focus on that, right? The reason why you understand is like, this for me is a front end funnel, right? If I had 1,000 people a week to register, after 52 weeks, I've got 52,000 people on a list over here. And if I'm just doing this, there's all this gold, all this money sitting on my list that I'm not doing anything with. So a couple of things, is number one, figure out other things to sell them, right? Like have other products, go back to some of my core training, like dot com secrets, how do you build a value ladder? What else can you serve in? How else can you sell cool products or services to them? So that's one thing, but the second thing is like, do I ever tell these people about this webinar again? And for a long time I didn't, cause I'm like, oh, they already watched the webinar, they already registered, that they would have wanted to watch it, they wouldn't, they didn't at the time. And I had a friend who, he came back and he told me one time, he said, look Russell, what I do is, once a quarter, I take my existing list, everyone who's ever subscribed to my webinar in the past, and do the webinar live for them again. And he's like, what's crazy, he's like, every single time I do that, I make an extra million bucks. I was like, what? Now his numbers are gonna be different for the years, obviously, but he was like, yeah, he's like, for some reason, like they didn't buy the first time, they weren't ready, it didn't make sense to them, they didn't show up, there's a million excuses why. So once a quarter I build up the whole funnel again, I clone it, I shoot a special promotion just for my existing list, I put them through this cycle, and boom, I make the same amount of money from that as I would from a typical webinar. And at first I didn't believe him, but I thought, you know, I'm gonna test it out. So I set up a webinar, the same one I've done a million times, I did it to my list, and sure enough, we crushed it on it. And so for me, I made a thing, okay, every quarter I'm doing the same webinar back to my same list, and consistently, like clockwork, we make a ton of money every single time we do it. I think a big part of it, I think to understand is I'll explain a concept, one of my friends is in the dating market, and he's a fascinating guy, and he sends out nine emails a day. And I think, why would you send out nine emails a day? And I asked him, I was like, dude, that's crazy. He said a couple things, he's like, for most men who are in the dating market, he's like, for the most part, most of the time they have a girlfriend, right? He's like, they stay on my list because I'm entertaining, so they're being entertained by me, they're laughing at stories, they're in a good mood, he's like, once or twice a year, they're gonna break up with their girlfriend. He says that, at that point, is when they're in pain, they're in emotional pain, he's like, I wanna make sure that when they're in that pain, when they pull their phone out, I wanna be the first email in their inbox. And it's fascinating, because a lot of times we think, oh, they didn't buy my thing, they must not be interested, it's like, no, they just weren't in enough pain in that period, right? For me, it's like when their business is struggling, or when they're ready to grow, or whenever something's happening, and they're in enough pain, that's when they're gonna buy it, right? For your product, they may not buy the first or second or third time, they may have been entertained going through the process, they're interested, but until they're in enough pain to make that decision, they're not ready. And so that's why I do the once a quarter thing with my existing list, because once a quarter, there's a new group of people who are in a certain amount of pain, enough pain that they're willing to take action, willing to start doing this thing now. And so that's why we open up once a quarter, because you never know when your audience is ready for what your message is right then, okay? And so that's kind of the process, you guys. It's not super complicated. There's a couple of pages, the first time will take you a little while to build up, then if you have it, it's just taking the funnel, and you click a button, you can clone it, set the new date, set the new times, re-plug everything, and then you have it again. Boom, do it again, just keep doing that process. Okay, so what I'm gonna do down below is we do these webinars all the time, I have a bunch of really cool templates we use, down below in the members area, I'll put a couple of these templates down here that you can use, and I'll gonna use you click a button, it'll take this entire template, it'll plug it into your ClickFunnels account, then you go in there and just change your headline, change your logos, change your pictures, you know, plug it into your Zoom account, change the emails, like you just kind of customize it to yourself, and then this funnel will become your funnel, it becomes really, really easy to do. So that's it for this training, you guys, thank you so much. I hope you see how you can translate this whole concept of webinars, and our stage presenting into webinars, and do the same thing online, and I promise you guys, there's something really nice about doing these things from the comfort of your home, and going back to bed at night with your own wife, and your own kids, your husbands, your spouses, your significant others, whatever it is for you. But for me, it was a game changer, it gave me the ability to continue to be a father, and a husband, and be a business owner, and not lose out on some of the most precious years of your children's lives, by being able to automate it and bringing it online. So, hope that helps. The share funnel links are down below for this webinar funnel. Hope you enjoy it, and hope you use this to grow your business, grow your following, and to change the world. Thanks so much.
